name: driftpak-body
description: Hardware interface for the Driftpak smart backpack. Controls camera, GPS, LEDs, audio, and device discovery via the Flask server running on localhost:5000.

tools.name: take_photo
tools.description: Capture a photo from the ESP32-S3-CAM. Returns a JPEG image. Use this to see what's around you or inside the bag.

tools.name: check_devices
tools.description: Ping all ESP32 devices and check which body parts are online.

tools.name: get_gps
tools.description: Get current GPS location (latitude, longitude, speed, satellites).

tools.name: led_on
tools.description: Turn on the LED strip on the bag strap. Use brightness 0-255.

tools.name: say
tools.description: Speak out loud through the EPOS speaker. Keep messages short and natural.

tools.name: listen
tools.description: Actively listen through the microphone for a specified duration and transcribe.

tools.name: get_transcripts
tools.description: Get recent speech transcripts from the continuous background listener. Poll this regularly to know what your owner is saying.

tools.name: list_audio_devices
tools.description: List all available audio input/output devices and which are active.

tools.name: scan_network
tools.description: Scan the local network for new ESP32 devices. New devices with /docs are auto-registered.

tools.name: list_registered_devices
tools.description: List all registered ESP32 devices and their capabilities.

tools.name: list_routes
tools.description: List all dynamic proxy routes for discovered devices.

tools.name: detect_objects
tools.description: Run fast YOLOv8 object detection on a camera capture. Identifies 80 common object types (backpack, bottle, phone, laptop, etc). Works offline.

tools.name: identify_scene
tools.description: Comprehensive scene identification using YOLO + Claude Vision. Recognizes everything: brands, text, keys, cables, context. Use for packing checks or detailed scene understanding. Optionally pass a question with ?prompt=...

tools.name: read_api_docs
tools.description: Read the full API documentation. Do this on startup to understand all available capabilities.
